name: Lakes of Nohèdes Loop
name_original: Nohèdes - le Gorg Estalat – boucle – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes
description: Hike the moderate 8.5-mile Nohèdes - le Gorg Estalat loop in Pyrénées Catalanes Regional Natural Park, featuring Lake Nohèdes and panoramic
region_intro: Bus station hiking trails around Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes traverse a diverse landscape, ranging from supramediterranean to subalpine zones. The region features varied geology, including the granitic Madres massif and limestone formations, creating a mosaic of habitats. Hikers encounter extensive forests, alpine meadows, rocky areas, and wetlands, with elevations spanning from 760m to 2459m. The Nohèdes River and its tributaries feed high-altitude lakes such as Estany del Clot and Gorg Estelat, adding to the area's natural features. Best bus station hiking trails around Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes The most popular bus station hiking route is Serrat Gran – Dolmen del Roc de Jornac loop from Urbanya, a 5.6 miles (9.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Roc de la Coste loop from Jujols, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path. This trail offers views of the surrounding mountains and forests, with a notable ascent. Local hikers also love the Jujols Orientation Table – Fonte Freda loop from Jujols, a 10.8 miles (17.3 km) trail leading through forests and open areas, often completed in about 6 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes is defined by diverse forests, high-altitude lakes, and varied geological formations, offering panoramic views of the Pyrenees. The network provides options for easy walks, moderate ascents, and challenging full-day routes. The routes in Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 700 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many bus station hiking trails are available around Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes?
A: There are over 60 hiking trails accessible from bus stations around the Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: Are there any easy bus-accessible hikes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, several easy routes are accessible by bus. A great option for families or beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35309198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jujols Orientation Table loop from Jujols&lt;/a&gt;. This easy hike offers pleasant views and is a good introduction to the area's natural beauty.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes from bus stations in the Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes?
A: Many of the trails accessible from bus stations in the Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes are circular, allowing you to start and end your hike at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35304602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrat Gran – Dolmen del Roc de Jornac loop from Urbanya&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35304756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roc de la Coste loop from Jujols&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on these bus-accessible hikes?
A: The Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes offers incredibly diverse terrain, ranging from 760m to 2459m in altitude. You can expect to traverse extensive forests, alpine lawns, heathlands, and rocky areas. Even within a few kilometers, you might experience rapid shifts in scenery and ecological zones. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24682001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jujols Orientation Table – Fonte Freda loop from Jujols&lt;/a&gt;, involve significant elevation gains, while easier trails have more gentle slopes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the bus-accessible trails?
A: Yes, many trails offer magnificent views. The Jujols Orientation Table, for instance, provides panoramic vistas of the Pyrenees, including the Pic Carlit and the Canigou massif, extending all the way to the Mediterranean Sea from higher points like the Pic des Tres Estelles. The region's diverse geology also features granite formations and limestone areas, creating captivating natural landmarks.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes using public transport?
A: The best time to hike generally depends on the specific trail and your preferences. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer is also popular, especially for higher altitude routes, but can be warmer. Winter hiking is possible, but some routes may be challenging due to snow and ice, requiring appropriate gear and experience.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails accessible by bus?
A: The Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes is a natural reserve, and regulations regarding dogs can vary. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and respect pastoral activities. Always check specific trail rules or local signage before heading out with your dog.

Q: Can I find any trails that pass by lakes or water features?
A: The reserve is known for its vital hydrological network, including the Nohèdes River and several high-altitude lakes. While specific bus-accessible routes passing directly by all these features may vary, the region's research highlights beautiful spots like Estany del Clot, Gorg Estelat, and Gorg Nègre, which are major attractions for hikers. Some longer, more challenging routes might lead to these serene locations.

Q: What do other hikers say about the bus-accessible trails in Réserve naturelle de Nohèdes?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in this region, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 800 ratings. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the natural environment, and the variety of trails available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging bus-accessible hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers looking for a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24682001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jujols Orientation Table – Fonte Freda loop from Jujols&lt;/a&gt; offer significant distance and elevation gain, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience through the reserve's rugged terrain.

Q: Are there any cafes or amenities near the bus stations or trailheads?
A: While the focus of the guide is on natural trails, some villages that serve as bus stops and trailheads may offer basic amenities like small shops or cafes. It's always advisable to check local information for specific villages like Jujols or Urbanya before your trip, and to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for longer hikes.
tips: Lake Nohèdes, also called Gorg Estelat, is a magnificent lake at an altitude of 2,002 meters at the foot of the Pic de Madrès.
Pretty lake located west of the Nohèdes nature reserve.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The Nohèdes - le Gorg Estalat loop typically takes around 4 hours and 46 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This is considered a moderate hike. It requires good physical condition and sure-footedness, as some sections can be steeper. It's classified as a mountain hike (SAC 2-3), meaning it may involve some exposure and a certain degree of mountaineering skill.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain, winding through picturesque beech and pine forests, and high pastures used for summer grazing. You'll hike on a mix of paths and forest tracks. There's also a fragile peat bog area at Pla del Gorg where it's important to stay on the designated path.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Nohèdes - le Gorg Estalat loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are permitted on this trail, but they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife and grazing animals.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The highlight of this loop is undoubtedly Lake Nohèdes (Gorg Estelat), a magnificent lake nestled at 2,002 meters. You'll also pass by Clot Lake and enjoy stunning panoramic views, including Mont Coronat and the majestic Canigó Massif.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in the Nohèdes Nature Reserve?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail within the Nohèdes Nature Reserve, which is part of the Pyrénées Catalanes Regional Natural Park. However, please respect the natural environment and follow all posted regulations.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Parking is available near the trailhead. Be aware that the final section of the access road leading to the trailhead can be challenging for some vehicles, so a car with high ground clearance is recommended.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this loop is generally from late spring through early autumn (May to October), when the weather is most favorable and the mountain lakes are accessible. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route does intersect with other paths. For instance, a small section of the trail follows the "Camí del Gorg" path. There are also longer and harder routes in the vicinity that lead to Lake Nohèdes, such as the "Clot Lake – Lake Nohèdes (Gorg Estelat) loop" for those seeking a greater challenge.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see in the Nohèdes Nature Reserve?
faqs.answer: The Nohèdes Nature Reserve is celebrated for its rich and varied biodiversity. While specific sightings are never guaranteed, you have a good chance of observing local flora and fauna, including various bird species and potentially some of the grazing animals in the high pastures.

faqs.question: Are there any specific environmental considerations I should be aware of?
faqs.answer: Yes, particularly around the fragile peat bog area at Pla del Gorg. It is crucial to remain on the designated path to protect this sensitive environment. Always practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the natural beauty of the reserve.
